&/usr/lib64/python3.9/importlib/util.pyr
z2Resolve a relative module name to an absolute one.
no package specified for z% (required for relative module names)r
startswith
ImportError
reprr
name
package
level
	characterr
resolve_name
Return the spec for the specified module.
    First, sys.modules is checked to see if the module was already imported. If
    so, then sys.modules[name].__spec__ is returned. If that happens to be
    set to None, then ValueError is raised. If the module is not in
    sys.modules, then sys.meta_path is searched for a suitable spec with the
    value of 'path' given to the finders. None is returned if no spec could
    be found.
    Dotted names do not have their parent packages implicitly imported. You will
    most likely need to explicitly import all parent packages in the proper
    order for a submodule to get the correct spec.
    N
{}.__spec__ is not set
{}.__spec__ is None)
modulesr
__spec__
AttributeError
ValueError
format)
path
module
specr
_find_spec_from_path*
Return the spec for the specified module.
    First, sys.modules is checked to see if the module was already imported. If
    so, then sys.modules[name].__spec__ is returned. If that happens to be
    set to None, then ValueError is raised. If the module is not in
    sys.modules, then sys.meta_path is searched for a suitable spec with the
    value of 'path' given to the finders. None is returned if no spec could
    be found.
    If the name is for submodule (contains a dot), the parent module is
    automatically imported.
    The name and package arguments work the same as importlib.import_module().
    In other words, relative module names (with leading dots) work.
    r
__path__)
fromlistz __path__ attribute not found on z
 while trying to find )
rpartition

NTF)

	Exception

	is_reloadr&
_module_to_loadv
zOSet __package__ on the returned module.
    This function is deprecated.
    c
7The import system now takes care of this automatically.
stacklevel

fxnr
set_package_wrapper
z(set_package.<locals>.set_package_wrapper
	functools
wraps)
set_package
zNSet __loader__ on the returned module.
    This function is deprecated.
    c
__loader__)
selfrF
set_loader_wrapper
z&set_loader.<locals>.set_loader_wrapperrK
set_loader
Decorator to handle selecting the proper module for loaders.
    The decorated function is passed the module to use instead of the module
    name. The module passed in to the function is either from sys.modules if
    it already exists or is a new module. If the module is new, then __name__
    is set the first argument to the method, __loader__ is set to self, and
    __package__ is set accordingly (if self.is_package() is defined) will be set
    before it is passed to the decorated function (if self.is_package() does
    not work for the module it will be set post-load).
    If an exception is raised and the decorator created the module it is
    subsequently removed from sys.modules.
    The decorator assumes that the decorated function takes the module name as
    the second argument.
    r;
is_packager
module_for_loader_wrapper
z4module_for_loader.<locals>.module_for_loader_wrapper)
module_for_loader
_LazyModulezKA subclass of the module type which triggers loading upon attribute access.c
z8Trigger the load of the module and return the attribute.
__dict__
	__class__z
module object for z. substituted in sys.modules during a lazy load)
